description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ruby:2.3 Docker image and define the variables CI_PROJECT_PATH_SLUG, DEPLOY_DOMAIN and DEPLOY_DOMAIN_APP. Define four stages: build, review, staging and production. In the build stage add a build job that runs on the jekyll tag, echoes "Build $CI_PROJECT_PATH_SLUG", cd web, ls -la, bundle install, ls -la, bundle exec jekyll build, mv _site ../public, cd .., ls -la, and publishes the public/ directory as an artifact. In the review stage add a review job that runs on branches except master, uses the gioxapp.com and deployctl tags, creates a dynamic review environment named review/$CI_BUILD_REF_NAME with a URL using $CI_ENVIRONMENT_SLUG, $CI_PROJECT_PATH_SLUG and $DEPLOY_DOMAIN_APP, and executes "deployctl --verbose --debug deploy". In the staging stage add a staging job that runs only on master, uses the gioxapp.com and deployctl tags, sets a staging environment with URL http://staging.$CI_PROJECT_PATH_SLUG.$DEPLOY_DOMAIN_APP, sets GIT_STRATEGY to none, and runs "deployctl deploy". In the production stage add a production job that runs only on master, is manual, uses the gioxapp.com and deployctl tags, sets a production environment with URL http://$DEPLOY_DOMAIN, and runs "deployctl deploy" followed by "deployctl conf_https". Also add stop_review and stop_staging manual jobs that run on the same tags, have GIT_STRATEGY none, stop their respective environments by running "deployctl delete", and are triggered manually (stop_review on branches except master, stop_staging on master).
